title: chenopodium ambrosioides var. suffruticosum
intro: Chenopodium ambrosioides var. suffruticosum, also known as Mexican tea or epazote, is a perennial herb native to Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects, as well as its ability to aid digestion and relieve menstrual cramps. The health benefits of Mexican tea include reducing symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, alleviating nausea and vomiting, and acting as a natural insect repellent. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant can be made into a tea, infusion, or tincture, and are often used to treat digestive issues and promote overall well-being.
